title: The run graph — the substrate's own provenance, as a SQL table
description: Where does all of this get stored? Every run the substrate executes persists a run record under .pi/bio-agent/runs/<runId>/run.json (spec, status, timestamps, an event log, and resolution receipts). This manifest reads those records back with DuckDB read_json, so the RUN GRAPH is itself a queryable table: the agent — or a UI — inspects its own provenance with the same SQL it uses for data. Nothing to plot from an opaque runtime; the provenance IS data. A grammar-of-graphics layer (posit's ggsql, https://github.com/posit-dev/ggsql) draws the run timeline straight off this table.

resolvers.title: SQL materialize
resolvers.description: Materialize a table from a read-only SELECT (here read_json over the run records).

params.sql: SELECT spec.id AS run_id, spec.tool.name AS tool, status, createdAt, len(events) AS event_count FROM read_json('.pi/bio-agent/runs/*/run.json', union_by_name = true, maximum_object_size = 100000000)
